AAPL.rationale: AAPL trades at a rich 31.4x P/E near the top of its 1-year range (20.5-33.0x) after a +48.5% one-year rally, with slowing revenue growth (FY rev $383B vs quarterly run-rate suggesting deceleration) and a 1.69x debt/equity ratio, leaving modest upside but limited margin of safety.

+48.5% 1-year return limits near-term upside
FY revenue $383.29B with quarterly trajectory showing deceleration (Q4 $89.5B vs Q3 $94.8B)
Strong fundamentals: 25.3% net margin, 156.1% ROE, 44.1% gross margin
Debt/equity 1.69x is elevated for a tech giant

WBA.rationale: Despite a 16.5% bounce off the lows, WBA's fundamentals remain deeply negative (FY23 net loss of $3.53B, operating margin -4.9%, ROE -12.5%) and the stock is still down 30% YoY, with no clear catalyst to reverse the structural earnings deterioration over a one-year horizon.

ROE of -12.5% and net margin of -2.5% signal value-destructive operations
Stock still down 30.3% YoY despite 16.5% 3-month bounce, suggesting the rebound is tactical not structural
P/B of 0.80x and P/S of 0.16x reflect cheap valuation but 'cheap can get cheaper' given negative earnings trajectory
News flow dominated by 'Dogs of the Dow' / worst-performer narratives with no positive catalysts

IBM.rationale: Reasonable valuation near the low end of its 1-year P/E range, strong ROE, and improving EPS support a mild long lean, but flat revenue and already-strong 3-month price action temper enthusiasm.
key_factors: P/E 21.8x near low end of 1-year range (19.3..104.5)
ROE 29.9% signals strong capital efficiency
EPS trajectory improving: $1.01 -> $1.72 -> $1.84 across Q1-Q3 2023
Revenue essentially flat at ~$14-15B/quarter (Q3 $14.75B vs Q2 $15.47B)
3-month return +16.2% already prices in much of the bullish AI narrative

sources_cited.excerpt: 3 Reasons to Buy IBM Stock in 2024
This Blue Chip Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Is a Buy for 2024

KO.rationale: Quality compounder at a fair (not cheap) multiple with improving EPS and strong sentiment support; modest upside with low downside risk warrants a mild long lean.
key_factors: Fair valuation: PE 23.7x sits mid-range of 1y band (21.6-29.2), EV/EBIT 25.0x mid-range (22.8-28.8) — not cheap, not stretched
Best-in-class fundamentals: ROE 38.7%, net margin 23.9%, operating margin 24.7% — durable quality compounder
Stable revenue with EPS recovery: Q3'23 rev $11.95B / EPS $0.71 vs Q2'23 EPS $0.59; FY22 EPS $2.19 anchors trajectory
Positive news flow: multiple 2024 buy recommendations, Buffett's $25B KO position highlighted, 'lapped the market' coverage
Price stabilizing: +6.2% over 3M after -7.8% over 1Y, last close $58.93 — momentum turning but not yet confirmed

sources_cited.excerpt: Warren Buffett Invested $25 Billion of His Portfolio in 2 Stocks That Could Rise 37% and 14% in 2024
Coca-Cola (KO) Laps the Stock Market: Here's Why
